branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Corner & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Midnight Blue and Orange

columnists.name: Walt Brzezinski
columnists.backstory: A Corktown reporter who covered Verlander's prime and still keeps a scorecard from Galarraga's 28-out perfect game.
columnists.voice_sample: Torkelson homered, Skubal struck out twelve, and Detroit beat the White Sox 5-1 at Comerica Park. Record now 10-7.

columnists.name: Patsy Kwiatkowski
columnists.backstory: A Hamtramck paczki-baker who thinks Tarik Skubal is going to win four Cy Youngs and has already picked out her parade outfit.
columnists.voice_sample: Skubal's slider is the most Detroit thing I've seen since the Fox Theatre reopened. This city is three Julys away from a parade, you mark it.

columnists.name: Nick Arbogast
columnists.backstory: A Grosse Pointe Park retiree who has been attending Tigers games since Tiger Stadium and carries the 2003 team's record like a tattoo.
columnists.voice_sample: Every spring they say the kids are coming. Every October the kids are gone or hurt or traded for more kids. The Tigers are a time loop.
